site_tagline: A FORTRAN-66 / DEC FORTRAN-10 interpreter, in Python.
site_description: Written in pure Python, with two pluggable axes: the front-end dialect — strict ANSI FORTRAN 66 by default, the DEC FORTRAN-10 superset on demand — and the machine value model, a 64-bit default or a 36-bit PDP-10. Conformance is checked against the FCVS corpus; the PDP-10 model, against the DEC FORTRAN-10 V5 manual and real 1970s code.
attribution: Software Architecture, Design & Engineering by Nicholas J. Kisseberth.
Code Synthesized via Anthropic Claude Code / Opus 4.8.
Automated Code Review via OpenAI Codex / ChatGPT 5.5.

docs.title: Language reference (FORTRAN 66)
docs.summary: The base dialect forterp implements: standard FORTRAN-66 plus the DEC FORTRAN-10 extensions, written for users of the interpreter — with the deliberate divergences spelled out.

docs.title: FORTRAN 77 reference
docs.summary: A complete, example-driven reference manual for the FORTRAN 77 language (ANSI X3.9-1978), organized on the standard: data types, arrays, expressions, control flow, I/O and FORMAT, procedures, and the intrinsic library — with a forterp notes box per chapter. This is the forterp.F77 dialect.

docs.title: Architecture & design
docs.summary: How it works inside and why: the source → lexer → parser → engine pipeline, the pluggable Target value model and Dialect axis, the memory/control models, and the seams that make it standalone.

docs.title: Command-line tools
docs.summary: The forterp / pyf66 / pyfortran10 commands: options, exit codes, multi-file linking, and the interactive monitor — RUN/LOAD, the per-statement debugger/profiler, and the IMMEDIATE REPL.

docs.title: Python API guide
docs.summary: Using forterp as a library: run_source / parse_source, the prebuilt interpreters and the Interpreter class, the target and dialect axes, embedding the Engine, the expert namespaces, custom host builtins, and the OOB census.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.summary: Dated history of the standalone interpreter, in order: the target/dialect axes, the CLI and monitor, the conformance work, and the review sweep.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.summary: Index of the forterp documentation and the authoritative standards it targets.
